name: Vulture (Find Dead Code in Python)

performance.notes: Static AST analysis; fast even for large codebases

agent_readiness.notes: Static analysis tool to find unused code. vulture src/ tests/. vulture --min-confidence 80 src/. Output: path/file.py:42: unused function 'my_func' (60% confidence). Whitelist: vulture whitelist.py src/ where whitelist.py contains dummy accesses. Python API: from vulture import Vulture. v = Vulture(). v.scavenge(['src/']). print(v.get_unused_code()). Pre-commit: pre-commit hook. Reports: unused variables, functions, classes, imports, attributes. False positive rate managed with --min-confidence.

gotchas: Dynamic code (getattr, __all__, plugins) generates false positives
Public APIs and entry points flagged as unused unless whitelisted
Confidence scores require tuning --min-confidence for each codebase
Whitelist file maintenance adds overhead for legitimate dynamic code
best_when: Cleaning up dead code before major refactors
CI checks to prevent accumulation of unused functions/variables
Code review support to identify code that can be deleted
Reducing technical debt in large Python codebases
avoid_when: Public libraries where API surface seems unused but isn't
Plugin-based architectures with heavy dynamic dispatch
When false positive rate from dynamic code patterns is too high
summary: Vulture finds dead/unused code in Python via AST analysis. vulture src/ reports unused functions, classes, variables, imports with confidence scores. --min-confidence 80 to filter likely false positives. Whitelist file for intentionally unused (API surfaces, plugins). Python API: Vulture().scavenge(['src/']). MIT. Fast and simple. Best for pre-refactor cleanup and CI dead-code checks. Complement ruff/flake8 (which don't detect unused functions/classes).
